description: A guarded pointer-walk can be decompiler residue for an ordinary counted loop.
The counted loop exposes the authored array shape and avoids preserving control flow solely because it currently matches.
Repeated adjacent assignments that only advance an index should be tested as a natural loop.
Preserving unrolled generated residue makes the source harder to review when a counted loop fits the local shape.
Repeated checks with the same exit condition are often an authored loop with an early return.
The loop keeps the control-flow intent visible instead of freezing four generated copies.
Local sibling helpers are stronger source-shape evidence than stack padding plus repeated generated calls.
Restore the authored helper boundary when nearby code shows the project used that boundary.
Nearby authored helper style can be stronger evidence than preserving bulky generated-looking collision logic.
The cleanup should fit the local item code shape rather than only mirror a decompiler output form.
A narrow objdiff benefit does not automatically make a source shape reviewable.
Prefer local analogs or a cleanup path over enshrining a tactic-shaped wrapper.

bad_pattern:
Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);
value = *(s32*) ((u8*) ip + 0xE38);
description: Recover item-var fields from the owning Item.xDD4 base, not from the first offset that happens to be used.
The typed union arm documents ownership and keeps later offsets from drifting.

bad_pattern:
/* grIceMt.c */
gp->gv.corneria.xC8 = 0;
description: Borrowing another stage family's union arm because offsets line up hides the owning stage type.
Use or add the GroundVars arm owned by the current map or stage family.
Raw byte arithmetic is the least reviewable bridge when a type is not recovered yet.
A real field is preferred; M2C_FIELD is a searchable temporary bridge while the owner type is still unknown.

preferred_shape:
HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj);
/* Or use the shared WithMtxDirty variant when that is the evidenced helper. */
description: Local fake helpers that mirror jobj.h asserts and writes should be compared against the owning header helper.
The fix path is to recover the canonical helper rather than copy the inline body into each TU.

bad_pattern:
jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj);
HSD_JObjSetTranslateX(jobj, x);
description: A familiar helper is not automatically the authored helper for every TU.
Use the local access form that matches surrounding source and objdiff evidence.
Expanded sibling helper bodies should be compared against a shared local inline/helper boundary.
Restoring a shared helper shape avoids leaving duplicated expanded logic in sibling functions.

preferred_shape: HSD_ASSERT(0x88, obj);
description: A direct assert block should become the project assert macro when file, line, condition, and side effects match.
Leave expanded forms only when the macro form would change object bytes or behavior.

preferred_shape:
HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x154, archive, "archive is null\n");
/* Preserve the report side effect while using the project macro form. */
description: Plain raw assert blocks are not the same as report-bearing assert blocks.
Use the macro family member that preserves message and report behavior.

preferred_shape:
HSD_ASSERT(1719, gp->gv.kongo.u.taru.keep);
/* Do not invent helper functions or data symbols just to feed assert text. */
description: A helper whose only purpose is to supply assert text is a generated/data-order tactic, not authored source style.
Use HSD_ASSERT for plain assertions; reserve HSD_ASSERTMSG for real custom messages with source evidence.

preferred_shape:
speed = 1.0F;
/* Keep named data only when symbol metadata and section ownership prove it. */
description: Introducing an extern, use, and definition together creates a data-order anchor instead of recovering source ownership.
Ordinary numeric constants should stay inline unless real data ownership is proven.
Address-named float statics that only stand in for ordinary values are fake data ownership.
The repair is to remove the fake data symbol use and keep the literal at the source site.
Inline literals are preferred only when there is no data ownership evidence.
When symbol and section evidence prove real storage, model that storage as typed data instead of flattening it away.

bad_pattern:
extern char mnNameNew_803EE38C[];

HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, mnNameNew_803EE38C);
preferred_shape:
HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, "MenMainBack_Top_joint");
/* Keep ordinary asset labels inline unless data ownership is the explicit scope. */
description: Asset and resource labels should not become address-style globals just to chase relocation or data layout.
This replacement makes a source literal look like a data-symbol ownership claim.
Archive section names are ordinary resource string literals at call sites.
Do not replace them with address-style string symbols just because the strings also exist in recovered data.

bad_pattern:
static char lbl_803EFB60[0xA8] = "Can't get user_data.\n\0\0\0";

OSReport(lbl_803EFB60 + 0x28);
preferred_shape:
OSReport("Can't get user_data.\n");
/* Recover a real evidenced string table only when source ownership proves it. */
description: Packed string blobs and offset accessors are data-ordering dodges when ordinary source strings should remain inline.
Keep the source string at the use site unless the change is explicitly proving string-table ownership.
Text and data status are different claims.
A code-matching change should not hide unresolved data layout behind source-level anchors or vague verification notes.
A helper that exists only to force literal order is data-matching work, not normal source cleanup.
Keep this kind of tactic narrow and evidence-bound instead of blending it into implementation style.

preferred_shape:
/* Land the verified text match first. */
/* Keep table reshaping in a data-scoped change with section evidence. */
description: Large table and storage reshaping can create data regressions even when nearby C looks cleaner.
The standard is checking whether data parity work is justified by the code-match scope.
Generated-looking dispatch chains should be checked against grouped switch forms.
This shape is goto residue; grouped switch dispatch is clearer and usually closer to authored source.

preferred_shape:
handpos.x = ABS(handpos.x);
/* Use the project macro when it expresses the source operation. */
description: Raw absolute-value branches should be tested against the project ABS macro.
Using the canonical macro avoids cloning local fabs-style helpers across unrelated files.

preferred_shape:
choice = random < 0.5F ? left : right;
/* Try the select expression when it is the natural source form. */
description: Select-like assignment code should be tested against a ternary expression.
The source form should stay readable while still matching the observed branch shape.
Manual UNUSED padding arrays are dummy locals posing as source structure.
When intentional padding is required, use PAD_STACK(N) and keep the tactic evidence-bound.
A dummy stack local is a tactic, not source structure.
If an authored helper boundary explains the stack shape, prefer the helper over permanent padding.
When a narrow .sdata2 ordering helper is accepted for a match, keep the tactic isolated from ordinary function logic and mark it as cleanup debt.
Use the project macro for the s32-to-f32 bias instead of spelling the raw double literal.
Raw inline assembly is not acceptable normal decomp source when a C implementation can be recovered.
Recover the C implementation instead of retaining an asm body for normal source.
The register keyword is a codegen-steering tactic and should not be introduced as routine source cleanup.
Reviewable source should remove register and similar tactic keywords unless a narrow exception is proven.
When a narrow pragma is accepted temporarily, describe it as cleanup debt rather than a permanent source fact.
A pragma exception should stay tightly scoped while preserving pressure to find the clean C form.
A real TU split needs source, build, split, and symbol ownership to move together.
Adjacent boundaries and local labels are part of the source cleanup, so splitting only the C file is incomplete.
Section ownership problems should usually be fixed in symbol or split metadata, not by inventing C storage.
Linkage or section issues belong in metadata; adding C storage hides the real ownership problem.
When code ownership moves, adjacent data ownership often moves with it.
Do not compensate for stale split metadata by leaving address-derived externs in the source.
Do not invent semantic names just because a function body became easier to understand.
Preserve address-style names when stronger naming evidence is not present.

bad_pattern:
static s32 lbl_804D6B10;
/* The value is known from map/debugger evidence but the source keeps an address label. */
preferred_shape:
static s32 g_debug_menu_cursor;
/* Use the semantic name when source, map, debugger, or callsite evidence proves the role. */
description: Conservative naming does not mean refusing semantic names forever.
Use the semantic name when the role is evidenced strongly enough to improve reviewability.

bad_pattern:
s32 temp_r30 = var_r4 + phi_f1;

return temp_r30;
description: Generated register names are decompiler residue.
Source should either use evidenced role names or preserve address-style globals when semantics are unknown.

bad_pattern:
#define HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize AXDriver_8038E034

size = HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize();
description: A canonical rename belongs in central symbol metadata or direct references, not in a source alias.
Perform the evidenced rename once instead of masking duplicate names with a define.

bad_pattern:
extern HSD_Archive* lbl_804D6A40; /* 804D6A40 */
extern HSD_Archive* menu_archive; /* 804D6A40 */
#define menu_archive lbl_804D6A40
preferred_shape:
extern HSD_Archive* lbl_804D6A40;
/* Or perform one evidenced scoped rename and update references directly. */
description: One address should not have multiple accepted source names hidden behind defines.
Duplicate externs and aliases make provenance harder to audit.
Macro-renaming declarations around an include hides a prototype conflict instead of fixing the owning header.
Repair the owning header signature and include it normally instead of using macro/prototype shims around the include.
When the body proves a signature, the owning header should stop advertising UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS.
Source recovery should improve the public declaration surface too.
When a recovered function body proves a pointer return, the prototype should expose that pointer type.
Generic storage may still need a local bridge, but that bridge should not become the semantic type of the function.

preferred_shape:
/* Verification note */
Verified exact: fn_8018846C, mnName_802388D4.
Not exact: mnName_GetColumnCount at 98.72 percent.
Next action: keep as improvement or repair before shipping.
description: Contradictory verification evidence should be preserved instead of collapsed into a match claim.
This workflow-only standard is checking the truthfulness of evidence, not C syntax.

preferred_shape:
Old unit: function_a moved to new TU
New unit: function_a exact
Old unit: function_b moved to new TU
New unit: function_b exact
/* Correlate split metadata before calling moved functions broken. */
description: When TUs are split, old-unit broken rows can be relocation/report artifacts.
Verification should correlate source ownership, split metadata, and new-unit matches before claiming regressions.

bad_pattern:
HSD_ASSERT(0x88, ptr);
/* This conversion changes object bytes, but it is kept anyway. */
description: A cleanup that changes object bytes should not be accepted just because the target style is desirable.
Keep non-byte-matching assert conversions expanded with a specific TODO until the byte-matching macro form is found.
